headline: 72-hour ceasefire methodology held cleanly — Russia's 6-event breach record complete with new 'strategic-format-exploitation' conduct category. New Day-3 Sustained-Conduct rule articulated (methodology v1.2). 4-day sequential backfill complete; series is current.
lead: The 72-hour US-brokered trilateral ceasefire expired May 11 with Russia breaching on all three days (6-event cumulative record: 3 unilateral collapses + 3 trilateral breaches killing 5 civilians and wounding 26, including a 14-year-old). ISW confirmed Russia used the ceasefire as a strategic stockpile window — pre-positioning Molniya drone systems and forward-moving reserves in the Lyman direction while the truce was nominally active. This 'strategic-format-exploitation' conduct category is new to the floor record. Ukraine maintained a breach-free posture across all 3 days and submitted a 1,000-prisoner exchange list; exchange not completed at expiry. A new methodology rule (v1.2) was articulated to handle sub-threshold dimensional pressure: carry-forward rather than discard. Hungary +9.4 proposal confidence upgraded to HIGH at 3-day inauguration window close. The 4-day sequential backfill (May 8-11) is complete; standard rotation resumes May 12.

confirmations.headline: Ukraine — CEASEFIRE WINDOW CLOSED. Confirmed at 50.0 Functional. No confirmed Ukrainian-attributed breach across all 3 trilateral ceasefire days. 1,000-prisoner list submitted; exchange not completed at expiry (coordination failure; list-submission earns partial ACT credit). Internal dimensional pressure +3.1 (ACT 4.0, INT 3.5; reconstructed 53.1). Sub-threshold; confirmed at 50.0. Dimensional credit carried forward per methodology v1.2.
watchItems: Ceasefire post-mortem: any extension/renewal of US-brokered framework

confirmations.scannerMislabelNote: Scanner described pending May 6 proposal as 'Functional → Established.' Actual May 6 proposal was downgrade to Developing. Mislabel documented; does not affect scoring record.
confirmations.headline: Waymo — Confirmed at 35.9 Developing. April 30 voluntary recall (3,791 vehicles, flooded-road software, OTA fix) and May 11 Koreatown rear-end crash (correctly attributed, no injuries) evaluated. Internal dimensional pressure +3.2 (transparency/governance). Sub-threshold; confirmed at 35.9. Dimensional credit carried forward per methodology v1.2. Band-crossing proposal from May 6 (Functional → Established) — new safety data is consistent with the proposal, not contradictory.
watchItems: Voluntary recall OTA fix deployment completion

confirmations.headline: Eswatini — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 9.4 / Critical confirmed. Absolute monarchy; political parties banned since 1973; pro-democracy protests suppressed (2021, lethal force); high HIV prevalence (>25% adults) structural EMP drag; gender inequality; media restrictions. Score of 9.4 consistent with consolidated authoritarian governance and absence of reform trajectory.

Lesotho — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 35.9 / Developing confirmed. Frequent coups and constitutional instability; high HIV/AIDS prevalence; extreme poverty (subsistence economy); domestic violence endemic; weak institutional capacity. Constitutional reform framework active 2025-2026. Conditional positive BND signals tempered by structural governance instability.

confirmations.boundaryNote: BOUNDARY WATCH — 2.4 above Critical floor (21.0). Press freedom restrictions, disputed elections, political instability, poverty. Any structural regression could trigger Critical band crossing.
confirmations.headline: Comoros — FIRST BASELINE. BOUNDARY WATCH. Composite 23.4 / Developing confirmed (2.4 above Critical floor). Disputed 2024 election; media freedom restrictions; political-opposition suppression; high poverty; limited institutional capacity. Score of 23.4 consistent with partial democratic functioning under persistent instability. Boundary watch active.
watchItems: Political stability and election integrity signals

confirmations.boundaryNote: BOUNDARY WATCH (internal reconstruction) — internal reconstruction places composite 0.9 below Functional floor. UPR cycle active. Climate displacement risk is structural EMP input.
confirmations.headline: Vanuatu — FIRST BASELINE. BOUNDARY WATCH (internal). Composite 35.9 / Developing confirmed; internal reconstruction 0.9 below Functional floor. Active UPR cycle. Frequent natural disasters (cyclones, earthquakes) structural EMP drag. Melanesian governance model; customary law coexists with statutory law. Political fragmentation (frequent government changes). Next positive enacted evidence could trigger Functional band crossing.
watchItems: UPR cycle recommendations and government response

confirmations.headline: Solomon Islands — FIRST BASELINE. Composite 35.9 / Developing confirmed. In-window UPR cycle. History of ethnic conflict (Tensions 1998-2003); domestic violence endemic; Chinese security agreement 2022 (BND dimension); climate vulnerability; limited institutional capacity. Published 35.9 consistent with evidence set.
watchItems: UPR cycle recommendations and government response

confirmations.bandPathCorrection: Developing → Developing (both 28.1 and 37.5 in band 21-40). Do NOT label as Critical → Developing.
confirmations.headline: Hungary — DAY-3 INAUGURATION WINDOW CLOSE — CONFIDENCE UPGRADED TO HIGH. Re-pin at 37.5 confirmed. 3-day window closed with no negative regression. 16 ministries constituted (Sulyok signed decrees May 12; sworn in May 13), including new standalone health, environmental protection, and education ministries. Sulyok has not resigned; May 31 deadline active. EU funds plan submission May 27 confirmed. Band: Developing → Developing (no band change; 28.1 and 37.5 both in band 21-40). June 9 is the 30-day re-assessment for Functional band crossing decision.
watchItems: May 27: EU funds plan submission (Magyar-von der Leyen summit)
May 31: Sulyok dismissal compliance deadline (ACC scoring trigger)
June 9: 30-day post-formation re-assessment — Functional band crossing decision (40.0+)

floorEntities.headline: Russia — FLOOR CONDUCT DOCUMENTATION — CEASEFIRE WINDOW CLOSED — DAY-3 FINAL. No score change. Day-3 breach confirmed: Kharkiv and Kherson strikes, 2 killed, 7 wounded including a 14-year-old. 38 Russian attacks reported by 16:00 (Ukrainian General Staff). ISW FINDING: Russia used the ceasefire as a strategic stockpile window — Molniya drone systems pre-positioned; reserves moved forward in Lyman direction; drone-strike tempo rose during truce. NEW CONDUCT CATEGORY: 'strategic-format-exploitation' — first application in floor-conduct record. Cumulative 6-event breach record: 3 unilateral collapses (May 5-7) + 3 trilateral breaches (days 1-2-3). Prisoner exchange: Russia claimed Ukraine not ready; disputed by Kyiv; exchange not completed. Floor at 0.0 sustained.
conductCategories: Day-3 strikes: 2 civilians killed, 7 wounded (incl. 14-year-old) in Kharkiv and Kherson during declared ceasefire (EMP/IHL)
38 confirmed attacks on Ukrainian positions May 11 per General Staff (IHL)
Strategic-format-exploitation (ISW): Molniya drone systems pre-positioned; reserves moved forward Lyman direction during truce window (INT — novel bad-faith category)
Drone-strike tempo rose during declared ceasefire per ISW assessment (IHL/INT)
Prisoner exchange blame-shifting: Putin accused Ukraine of unreadiness; disputed by Kyiv; exchange not completed (ACT/INT)
MoD continued 'strict observance' claims while strikes proceeded — bad-faith-INT pattern, day 3 of 3 (INT)
Cumulative 6-event breach record complete: 3 unilateral collapses (May 5-7) + Kharkiv May 9 + Ukraine-wide May 10 + Kharkiv/Kherson May 11

floorEntities.headline: Israel — FLOOR CONDUCT DOCUMENTATION — DAY-3 CLOSE. No score change. OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations (35 flotilla detainees, May 8 press release) remain unaddressed and unresolved at day-3 close. Gaza confirmed killed: 72,740+ (20,179 children) as of May 11. UNRWA access block since March 2025 ongoing. UNCLOS jurisdiction questions (April 29 interception, 500 nm from Israeli coast) unresolved. Deportation of final detained activists (May 10) resolved detention-duration only — not accountability. Floor at 0.0 sustained.
conductCategories: OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations — 35 flotilla detainees; May 8 documentation; unaddressed and unresolved at day-3 close (UNCAT)
Gaza confirmed casualties: 72,740+ killed (20,179 children) as of May 11 — accumulation ongoing (EMP/IHL)
UNRWA access block ongoing since March 2025 — systematic humanitarian-access restriction (EMP/humanitarian law)
UNCLOS international-waters jurisdiction — April 29 interception 500 nm from Israeli coast; no accountability proceedings (BND/IHL)
Flotilla arbitrary-detention phase CLOSED by deportation (May 10) — not by accountability proceedings; accountability gap documented

mathHygiene.note: Math-hygiene cluster unchanged at 13 entities. All cycle counts incremented by 1. Waymo NOT added to cluster — score change is real (evidence-based reassessment), not a reconstruction issue. Scanner mislabel (Waymo described as 'Functional → Established' pending) documented; actual May 6 proposal is downgrade to Developing. Formula audit (robotics-labs + ai-labs) must move to in-progress immediately.

criticalFlag.severity: CRITICAL — 9 cycles carried; most senior unresolved item in system. Published score at active risk of external citation as valid benchmark data point. Formula audit must move from flagged to in-progress before next robotics-labs publication cycle.

mathHygiene.auditAction: MANDATORY: Cross-index formula audit (robotics-labs + ai-labs composite-derivation formulas) must move from flagged to in-progress before next robotics-labs publication cycle. Open Bionics 9-cycle carry is a publication-integrity obligation, not a maintenance item.

methodologyUpdates.name: Day-3 Sustained-Conduct Dimensional-Credit-Under-Sub-Threshold-Composite Rule
methodologyUpdates.ruleText: When sustained good-faith conduct over a defined observable window produces measurable upward dimensional pressure that nonetheless reconstructs to a composite delta below the 5.0pt materiality threshold, the dimensional credit is carried forward as documented dimensional pressure rather than applied as a material composite upgrade.
operationalConsequences: Sub-threshold dimensional pressure is preserved in the entity scoring record, not discarded
Carry-forward credit is visible to future assessors and may combine with new evidence to cross the materiality threshold in a later cycle
Prevents systematic bias where entities making steady moderate improvements receive no scoring recognition
